title: Quiz: ADAPT-VQE Fundamentals
description: Test your understanding of the ADAPT-VQE algorithm and its key concepts

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of ADAPT-VQE over traditional UCCSD ansatze?

options.text: ADAPT-VQE always converges faster
ADAPT-VQE builds compact, problem-tailored circuits with fewer parameters
ADAPT-VQE requires no classical optimization
ADAPT-VQE works only for hydrogen molecules

questions.explanation: ADAPT-VQE's main advantage is constructing compact, problem-specific ansatze by adding only the operators that significantly contribute to lowering the energy, typically requiring 10-100x fewer parameters than UCCSD.

questions.question: How does ADAPT-VQE select which operator to add at each iteration?

options.text: Randomly from the operator pool
Based on the operator with the largest gradient magnitude
In sequential order from the pool
Based on circuit depth considerations only

questions.explanation: ADAPT-VQE selects the operator with the largest gradient magnitude |∂E/∂θ|_{θ=0}, which indicates the operator that would most effectively reduce the energy when added to the ansatz.

questions.question: What is the gradient formula used in ADAPT-VQE for operator selection?

options.text: ⟨ψ|H|ψ⟩
⟨ψ|A|ψ⟩
⟨ψ|H·A|ψ⟩

questions.explanation: The gradient ∂E/∂θ|_{θ=0} equals ⟨ψ|[H, A]|ψ⟩, the expectation value of the commutator between the Hamiltonian and the candidate operator.

questions.question: What is an 'operator pool' in ADAPT-VQE?

options.text: The set of qubits used in the algorithm
A predefined collection of operators from which ADAPT-VQE selects
The classical optimization parameters
The measurement basis choices

questions.explanation: The operator pool is a predefined collection of anti-Hermitian operators (like excitation operators) from which ADAPT-VQE iteratively selects the most impactful ones to build the ansatz.

questions.question: In ADAPT-VQE, once an operator is added to the ansatz, its parameter remains fixed throughout the algorithm.

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: False. After each operator is added, ALL parameters in the ansatz are re-optimized together using VQE. This ensures the best possible energy for the current circuit structure.

questions.question: What is the convergence criterion for ADAPT-VQE?

options.text: When energy reaches exactly zero
When maximum gradient magnitude falls below a threshold
When all operators from the pool have been used
After a fixed number of iterations

questions.explanation: ADAPT-VQE converges when max|∂E/∂θᵢ|_{θᵢ=0} < ε, meaning no remaining operator can significantly reduce the energy. Typical thresholds are 10⁻³ to 10⁻⁵ Hartree.

questions.question: What is Qubit-ADAPT?

options.text: A version of ADAPT-VQE that uses more qubits
A version using individual Pauli strings instead of fermionic operators
A classical simulation of ADAPT-VQE
A post-processing technique for ADAPT-VQE

questions.explanation: Qubit-ADAPT uses individual Pauli strings as pool operators instead of fermionic excitation operators, resulting in shallower circuits per operator while maintaining chemical accuracy.

questions.question: Which of the following are limitations of fixed UCCSD ansatze that ADAPT-VQE addresses? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Over-parameterization with many negligible operators
Circuit depths exceeding NISQ device capabilities
System-agnostic structure regardless of the molecule
Inability to represent any quantum state

questions.explanation: ADAPT-VQE addresses: (a) over-parameterization by selecting only impactful operators, (b) deep circuits by building compact ansatze, and (c) system-agnostic design by tailoring to specific molecules. UCCSD can represent relevant states, so (d) is not a limitation.
